req.title: Biller registration
req.statement: A customer shall register a biller only after a 24-hour hold; withdrawals to new billers shall be deferred.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-023 defines three testable behaviours: (1) a valid biller registration lands in 'hold' state (positive / EP), (2) registering the same biller twice returns HTTP 409 (negative / NEG), (3) the hold period boundary – attempting a withdrawal exactly at T=0 hours after registration must be deferred while at T=24h it must succeed (boundary / BVA). A fourth STC case traces the full hold→active state transition to ensure the biller becomes usable after the hold expires. Each test is self-contained via an authenticated client fixture and a unique biller-ID generator so repeated runs leave the system clean.

tests.name: test_register_new_biller_enters_hold_state
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid JWT present in fixture)
Biller does not already exist for this customer
actions: Generate a unique biller payload via <make_biller_payload>
POST /billers with payload and auth header
trace: R-FIN-023: biller shall be placed in hold state upon registration
R-FIN-023: withdrawals to new billers shall be deferred
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_register_new_biller_enters_hold_state(auth_client, make_biller_payload):
    payload = make_biller_payload(biller_id=str(uuid.uuid4()))
    resp = auth_client.post("/billers", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("status") == "hold", f"Expected 'hold', got {body.get('status')}"
    assert "biller_id" in body, "biller_id missing from response"
    assert body.get("withdrawal_allowed", False) is False, "Withdrawals must be deferred"

tests.name: test_duplicate_biller_registration_returns_409
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid JWT present in fixture)
Biller with target biller_id already registered for this customer
actions: Generate a unique biller payload via <make_biller_payload>
POST /billers again with identical payload (duplicate attempt)
Capture HTTP response of second call
expected: Response body contains error code or message indicating duplicate biller
trace: R-FIN-023: error pathway – 409 if duplicate biller
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_duplicate_biller_registration_returns_409(auth_client, make_biller_payload):
    biller_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = make_biller_payload(biller_id=biller_id)
    first = auth_client.post("/billers", json=payload)
    assert first.status_code == 201, f"Setup failed: {first.status_code} {first.text}"
    second = auth_client.post("/billers", json=payload)
    assert second.status_code == 409, f"Expected 409, got {second.status_code}: {second.text}"
    body = second.json()
    assert "duplicate" in str(body).lower() or "conflict" in str(body).lower(), (
        f"Expected duplicate/conflict error message, got: {body}"
    )

tests.name: test_withdrawal_deferred_at_hold_boundary_zero_hours
preconditions: Biller was just registered (T=0 h, squarely inside 24-hour hold window)
System clock is not mocked; biller created within this test
actions: Register a new biller via POST /billers
Immediately attempt a withdrawal to that biller via POST /withdrawals
Capture HTTP response of withdrawal attempt
expected: Immediate POST /withdrawals returns HTTP 422 or 403 indicating deferred/not-allowed
Response body references hold period or deferred withdrawal
trace: R-FIN-023: withdrawals to new billers shall be deferred (boundary: T=0h, minimum hold time)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_withdrawal_deferred_at_hold_boundary_zero_hours(auth_client, make_biller_payload, make_withdrawal_payload):
    biller_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
    reg_resp = auth_client.post("/billers", json=make_biller_payload(biller_id=biller_id))
    assert reg_resp.status_code == 201, f"Registration failed: {reg_resp.text}"
    assert reg_resp.json().get("status") == "hold"
    withdrawal = make_withdrawal_payload(biller_id=biller_id, amount=1.00)
    w_resp = auth_client.post("/withdrawals", json=withdrawal)
    assert w_resp.status_code in (403, 422), (
        f"Expected deferred withdrawal (403/422), got {w_resp.status_code}: {w_resp.text}"
    )
    body_str = str(w_resp.json()).lower()
    assert "hold" in body_str or "defer" in body_str or "not allowed" in body_str, (
        f"Response did not indicate hold/deferred: {w_resp.json()}"
    )

tests.name: test_biller_transitions_from_hold_to_active_after_24h
preconditions: Application supports time-override header or test backdating endpoint <set_biller_registration_time>
Biller registered and its creation timestamp backdated to 25 hours ago
actions: Backdate registration timestamp by 25 hours via <set_biller_registration_time> test-support helper
GET /billers/{biller_id} to confirm status has transitioned
Optionally trigger activation job via <trigger_hold_expiry_job> if status is still 'hold'
expected: After backdating and optional job trigger, GET /billers/{biller_id} returns status='active'
withdrawal_allowed is true for active biller
trace: R-FIN-023: 24-hour hold – biller must leave hold state after hold period expires
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_biller_transitions_from_hold_to_active_after_24h(
    auth_client, make_biller_payload, set_biller_registration_time, trigger_hold_expiry_job
):
    biller_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
    reg = auth_client.post("/billers", json=make_biller_payload(biller_id=biller_id))
    assert reg.status_code == 201
    assert reg.json().get("status") == "hold"
    set_biller_registration_time(biller_id=biller_id, hours_ago=25)
    trigger_hold_expiry_job()
    get_resp = auth_client.get(f"/billers/{biller_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200, f"GET failed: {get_resp.text}"
    body = get_resp.json()
    assert body.get("status") == "active", f"Expected 'active', got {body.get('status')}"
    assert body.get("withdrawal_allowed") is True, "Withdrawal must be allowed after hold expires"
